🧠

How to play: Memory Matrix

Memorize the lit squares, then tap them in any order!

  • 👀
    Watch the squares light up — remember ALL of them!
  • 👆
    When the grid goes dark, tap every square that was lit
  • ✅
    Tap all correct squares = round complete
  • ⬆️
    Each round adds one more square to remember
  • 🏆
    Score = total correct squares across all rounds
🔬 Science: Corsi Block-Tapping Task (1972) — tests visuospatial working memory
ℹ️
🧠 Memory0
🃏

How to play: Card Match

Find all matching pairs as fast as possible!

  • 👀
    All cards start face-down
  • 👆
    Tap any card to flip it over
  • 🔄
    Tap a second card — if they match, both stay flipped!
  • ❌
    No match? Both cards flip back after 0.8s
  • ✅
    Flip ALL pairs to win! Fewer moves = better score
🔬 Science: Paired Associates Learning (CANTAB, Owen et al. 1995)
ℹ️
🃏 Card Match0
🔔

How to play: Simon Says

Repeat the color sequence — it grows each round!

  • 👀
    Watch the colors light up in order — remember the sequence!
  • 👆
    Tap the colors in the SAME ORDER you just saw
  • ➕
    Each round adds one more color to the sequence
  • ❌
    Tap wrong? Round ends — but you earn points for completed rounds
  • 🏆
    Complete all 6 rounds to max out!
🔬 Science: Miller's Law: humans hold 7±2 chunks in working memory (1956)
ℹ️
🔔 Simon SaysR1/5
➗

How to play: Math Sprint

Solve 10 math problems as fast as you can!

  • 🔢
    A math problem appears — pick the correct answer from 4 options
  • ⏱️
    You have about 13 seconds per question — the bar shrinks!
  • ⚡
    Faster correct answers = higher score
  • ❌
    Time runs out? It counts as wrong, next question
  • 🏆
    10/10 in under 3 seconds each = top 1%!
🔬 Science: WRAT-4 Math Fluency (Wilkinson, 2006) — numerical processing speed
ℹ️
➗ Math Sprint0/10
🎨

How to play: Stroop Test

Does the WORD match its ink COLOR?

  • 👀
    A color word appears (e.g. RED) written in some ink color
  • ✓
    Tap MATCH if the word AND ink color are the same (e.g. RED in red ink)
  • ✗
    Tap NO if they are different (e.g. RED in blue ink)
  • ⚡
    Be fast AND accurate — your brain wants to read the word!
  • 🧠
    This tests your ability to IGNORE automatic responses
🔬 Science: Stroop Color-Word Test (1935) — measures response inhibition, anterior cingulate cortex
ℹ️
🎨 Stroop Test0/12
Does the WORD match its INK color?
⚡

How to play: Reaction Time

Tap the GREEN circle as fast as possible!

  • 🔴
    Wait for the circle — it starts RED
  • 🟢
    When it turns GREEN — tap it IMMEDIATELY!
  • ⚠️
    Tap too early (before green)? +900ms penalty!
  • 📊
    Your time in milliseconds is recorded each round
  • 🏆
    Under 200ms = elite athlete level!
🔬 Science: Simple Reaction Time (Jensen, 1979) — correlates r=0.40 with general intelligence
ℹ️
⚡ Reaction TimeR1/5
🌈

How to play: Color Rush

Tap the button that matches the named color!

  • 👀
    The top shows a color name (e.g. BLUE)
  • 👆
    Tap the button WITH THAT COLOR — not the word, the actual background!
  • ⚡
    Go as fast as you can — speed matters
  • 12
    12 questions total
  • 🏆
    12/12 fast = color processing champion!
🔬 Science: Stroop (1935) + Farnsworth-Munsell color discrimination — V4 cortex processing
ℹ️
🌈 Color Rush0/12
💭

How to play: Number Memory

Memorize digits, then type them back!

  • 👀
    A sequence of digits appears — memorize ALL of them!
  • ⏱️
    They disappear after a few seconds — shorter sequences hide faster
  • 🔢
    Type the digits back using the number pad
  • OK
    Hit OK when done — must be EXACT!
  • ⬆️
    Each level adds one more digit! How far can you go?
🔬 Science: Digit Span Forward — WAIS-IV (Wechsler, 2008) — average adult span: 7 digits
ℹ️
💭 Number MemoryLv 1
👁

How to play: Odd One Out

Tap the shape that is DIFFERENT!

  • 🔍
    A grid of shapes appears — one of them is different
  • 🎨
    The odd one might have a different COLOR, SIZE, or ROTATION
  • 👆
    Tap the shape that does NOT match the others
  • ⬆️
    Grids get larger in later rounds
  • 🏆
    8/8 = eagle eyes!
🔬 Science: Visual Discrimination — NEPSY-II (Korkman et al. 2007) — inferior temporal cortex
ℹ️
👁 Odd One Out0/8
Tap the one that's different
🔢

How to play: Sequence

What number comes next in the pattern?

  • 🔍
    Look at the sequence of numbers: 2 · 4 · 8 · ?
  • 🧠
    Figure out the rule: add? multiply? alternate?
  • 👆
    Pick the correct next number from 4 options
  • ⏱️
    No time limit — think carefully!
  • 🏆
    8/8 = top fluid intelligence score!
🔬 Science: Raven's Progressive Matrices analog — loads 0.82 on g factor (Carroll, 1993)
ℹ️
🔢 Sequence0/8
What comes next?
✨

How to play: Flash Count

How many dots appeared?

  • 👀
    Dots appear on screen for less than 1 second — watch closely!
  • 💨
    They disappear very fast!
  • 👆
    Pick how many dots you saw from 4 options
  • ⬆️
    More dots appear in later rounds
  • 🏆
    8/8 = subitizing superstar!
🔬 Science: Subitizing (Kaufman et al. 1949) — pre-attentive numerosity, right intraparietal sulcus
ℹ️
✨ Flash Count0/8
🔤

How to play: Word Scramble

Unscramble the letters to make a word!

  • 🔀
    Scrambled letters appear at the top
  • 🔠
    Tap letters in the correct order to spell the word
  • ⌫
    Made a mistake? Tap the backspace button
  • 💡
    Hint: all words are common English vocabulary
  • 🏆
    8/8 = language master!
🔬 Science: Boston Naming Test analog (Kaplan, 2001) — left hemisphere lexical access
ℹ️
🔤 Word Scramble0/8
Unscramble the word
🔄

How to play: N-Back

Tap MATCH when a symbol repeats from the PREVIOUS one!

  • 👀
    Watch symbols appear one at a time
  • 🔁
    Tap MATCH! when the symbol is the SAME as the one BEFORE it
  • ❌
    Do NOT tap if it is a new or different symbol
  • ⚡
    Symbols change every 1.1 seconds — stay focused!
  • 🧠
    This is one of the hardest working memory tests!
🔬 Science: N-Back Task (Jaeggi et al. 2008 PNAS) — trains working memory and fluid intelligence
ℹ️
🔄 N-Back0
🎯

How to play: Speed Tap

Tap the golden orb as fast as possible!

  • 👀
    A glowing golden orb appears somewhere on the screen
  • 👆
    Tap it as FAST as you can!
  • 💨
    It disappears immediately when you tap
  • 📊
    Your average reaction time is tracked over 10 orbs
  • 🏆
    Under 380ms average = top speed!
🔬 Science: Fitts' Law (1954) + Halstead-Reitan Motor Speed — cerebellar-motor loop
ℹ️
🎯 Speed Tap0/10
🔗

How to play: Word Pairs

Memorize word pairs, then recall them!

  • 👀
    4 word pairs appear for 5 seconds — memorize ALL of them!
  • ❓
    One word from a pair is shown — which word was it paired with?
  • 👆
    Pick the correct partner from 4 options
  • 💡
    Try to create a mental image linking the two words!
  • 🏆
    6/6 = exceptional verbal memory!
🔬 Science: Verbal Paired Associates — WMS-IV (Wechsler, 2009) — hippocampal episodic memory
ℹ️
🔗 Word Pairs0/6
🔍

How to play: Visual Search

Find the target symbol hidden in the grid!

  • 🎯
    The target symbol is shown at the top (e.g. 6)
  • 🔍
    Find it hidden among distractor symbols (e.g. 9)
  • 👆
    Tap the cell that contains the target
  • ⬆️
    The grid gets bigger in later rounds!
  • 🏆
    8/8 fast = top visual attention!
🔬 Science: Feature Integration Theory (Treisman & Gelade 1980) — FEF + IPS attention network
ℹ️
🔍 Visual Search0/8
🧪

    🧠

    Train Your Brain Daily

    51 science-based games across memory, focus, math, logic, language and speed — adapts to your level.

    Track Your Brain Score

    Your personal EPQ score rises as you improve — see exactly where to focus next.

    ♾️

    Ages 4 to 99. Never Repeats.

    Family plan for every mind. Procedural generation means you'll never play the same session twice.

    03Screenshots App Store — iPhone 10 telas

    04Changelog v8

    • Botão ⏸️ pause em TODAS as telas: sempre visível no canto superior direito — Resume, Home, Games, Tests, Mute, Share, Quit
    • Fundo 4K do cérebro sincronizado entre app e screenshots: gerado via Higgsfield GPT Image 2 4K alta qualidade
    • Brain BG nas stages dos jogos: cada jogo tem fundo de cérebro com opacity controlada para não distrair
    • Brain BG no onboarding: 4 slides com fundo de cérebro animado
    • Animações em todo o app: float nos emojis dos jogos e testes, pulse no streak, bounce nos cards, shimmer nos carregamentos
    • Screenshots restauradas para ss_v2 (a versão boa anterior) — eliminada ss_v3 (horível)
    • 51 jogos completos: 16 totalmente jogáveis, restantes desbloqueáveis via Premium
    • 10 testes: IQ + 9 awareness com questões procedurais anti-repeat
    FlowIQ · com.rafael.flowiq · v8 · 13/jun/2026
